T1.name: Reconnaissance
T1.description: Activities conducted prior to active intrusion to gather intelligence on target infrastructure, personnel, and security posture.
sub_techniques: T1.1 — Active Scanning (honeypot telemetry + anomaly threshold models)
T1.2 — Passive OSINT Aggregation (NLP entity extraction from dark web)
T1.3 — Spearphishing Victim Profiling (email metadata graph analysis)
T1.4 — Infrastructure Enumeration (DNS passive replication + BGP monitoring)

T2.name: Resource Development
T2.description: Adversary activities to establish operational infrastructure required to conduct an attack campaign.
sub_techniques: T2.1 — Malicious Domain Acquisition (WHOIS temporal analysis + DGA pattern detection)
T2.2 — Custom Implant Development (malware genome similarity hashing)
T2.3 — Infrastructure Staging (ASN clustering + certificate transparency log mining)

T3.name: Initial Access
T3.description: The vector by which an adversary achieves first foothold within the target environment. Most extensively studied phase (89/100 corpus papers).
sub_techniques: T3.1 — Spearphishing (email header analysis + URL detonation sandbox)
T3.2 — Exploit Public-Facing Application (CVE-signature matching + WAF log anomaly)
T3.3 — Supply Chain Compromise (SBOM integrity verification)
T3.4 — Valid Account Abuse (UBA deviation + authentication log analysis)
T3.5 — Watering Hole Attack (browser exploit kit fingerprinting + JS deobfuscation)

T4.name: Execution
T4.description: Techniques by which adversaries run malicious code. LOLBin abuse is dominant pattern from 2022 onward.
sub_techniques: T4.1 — PowerShell/Script Interpreter Abuse (AST-based analysis + AMSI telemetry)
T4.2 — Scheduled Task/Job Execution (task scheduler log mining + temporal graph)
T4.3 — LOLBin Abuse (signed binary execution anomaly scoring)
T4.4 — User-Triggered Execution (document macro extraction + sandbox detonation)

T5.name: Persistence
T5.description: Mechanisms maintaining foothold. Highest inter-group discriminative power: 88.3-93.7% attribution accuracy.
sub_techniques: T5.1 — Registry Run Key Modification (registry delta monitoring + baseline comparison)
T5.2 — Web Shell Deployment (HTTP log anomaly + file hash monitoring)
T5.3 — Boot/Logon Autostart (MBR/UEFI integrity verification)
T5.4 — Account Manipulation/Backdoor Account (IAM audit log correlation)

T6.name: Privilege Escalation
T6.description: Techniques for gaining elevated permissions. Automated detection via exploit chain sequencing and token manipulation signatures.
sub_techniques: T6.1 — Kernel Exploit Deployment (CVE-to-syscall signature mapping)
T6.2 — Access Token Manipulation (Windows Security Event log anomaly scoring)
T6.3 — Sudo/SUID Binary Abuse (Linux auditd log correlation)

T7.name: Defense Evasion
T7.description: Most technique-rich MITRE tactic. Multi-label classification required; GNNs achieve F1=0.871 for composite evasion detection.
sub_techniques: T7.1 — Obfuscated File/Code Execution (entropy analysis + ML deobfuscation)
T7.2 — Indicator Removal/Log Clearing (log integrity verification + shadow copy monitoring)
T7.3 — Rootkit/Firmware Implant (memory forensics + UEFI firmware hash verification)
T7.4 — Timestomping (MFT $STANDARD_INFORMATION vs $FILE_NAME delta analysis)
T7.5 — Process Injection (memory region anomaly scoring + thread injection graph)

T8.name: Credential Access
T8.description: Techniques targeting authentication material. Enables lateral movement without triggering behavioral anomalies.
sub_techniques: T8.1 — LSASS Memory Dumping (WDigest registry monitoring + LSASS access call graph)
T8.2 — Kerberoasting/AS-REP Roasting (Kerberos TGS request frequency anomaly)
T8.3 — Credential Stuffing/Password Spray (authentication failure temporal clustering)

T9.name: Discovery
T9.description: Internal reconnaissance post-compromise. Maps target environment, identifies high-value assets.
sub_techniques: T9.1 — Network/Service Scanning (internal NetFlow anomaly + port scan signature matching)
T9.2 — Active Directory Enumeration (LDAP query volume anomaly + BloodHound-pattern detection)
T9.3 — System Information Discovery (WMI/PowerShell query clustering)

T10.name: Lateral Movement
T10.description: Modelled as graph traversal problem. HMMs and GNNs are highest-performing architectures. Temporal ordering is most critical input.
sub_techniques: T10.1 — Pass-the-Hash/Pass-the-Ticket (NTLM authentication anomaly + Kerberos ticket reuse)
T10.2 — Remote Service Exploitation RDP/SMB (lateral connection graph + HMM)
T10.3 — Internal Spearphishing (internal email metadata analysis)
T10.4 — Software Deployment Tool Abuse (SCCM/GPO change monitoring)

T11.name: Collection
T11.description: Activities identifying, aggregating, and staging data prior to exfiltration.
sub_techniques: T11.1 — Data Staged for Exfiltration (temporary directory write volume spike detection)
T11.2 — Screen/Input Capture (kernel hook detection + accessibility API abuse monitoring)
T11.3 — Email/Repository Collection (Exchange audit log anomaly + mass mailbox access)

T12.name: Command & Control (C2)
T12.description: Most studied node (91/100 papers). Clear arms race: DNS/HTTP C2 (2020-21) → encrypted C2 (2022-23) → legitimate-service-abusing C2 (2024-26).
sub_techniques: T12.1 — Domain Fronting (CDN request header inconsistency analysis)
T12.2 — DNS Tunneling (query length/entropy statistical model)
T12.3 — HTTPS Beacon Traffic (JA3/JA3S TLS fingerprinting + beacon interval clustering)
T12.4 — Steganographic C2 (image/media payload entropy analysis + LSB detection)
T12.5 — Legitimate Service Abuse Slack/GitHub/OneDrive (API call pattern deviation)

T13.name: Exfiltration
T13.description: Low-and-slow exfiltration is most significant detection challenge. APT groups fragment transfers to remain below DLP thresholds.
sub_techniques: T13.1 — Exfiltration Over C2 Channel (volume-time series anomaly on established C2 sessions)
T13.2 — Exfiltration to Cloud Storage (authorized cloud upload volume baseline deviation)
T13.3 — Physical Medium Exfiltration (USB device registry correlation + DLP endpoint)
T13.4 — Scheduled Transfer low-and-slow (DBSCAN + time-series decomposition)

T14.name: Impact
T14.description: Actions to manipulate, disrupt, or destroy. Not in all APT campaigns; present in groups with destructive mandates (Sandworm, APT38).
sub_techniques: T14.1 — Data Destruction/Wiper Deployment (file system entropy spike + MBR integrity monitoring)
T14.2 — Ransomware Deployment (file extension mass-rename detection + shadow copy deletion)
T14.3 — OT/ICS System Manipulation (PLC command sequence anomaly + Purdue model deviation)
